---
title: "Contact Tracing"
author: "Simon Frost"
date: today
subtitle: "NetworkOutbreaks.jl Vignette 04"
engine: julia
julia:
  project: ".."
---

## Introduction

Contact tracing quarantines the contacts of detected infectious
individuals. NetworkOutbreaks.jl models this using `:contact_trace`
transitions — a rate-based neighbour-dependent mechanism that preserves
Gillespie correctness by treating each quarantine as an elementary SSA event.

## Setup

```{julia}
using NetworkOutbreaks
using Graphs
using StableRNGs
using Statistics
using Plots
```

## SIRQ model with contact tracing

Susceptible individuals are quarantined (S → Q) at a rate proportional
to the number of infectious neighbours they have. The `via` keyword
specifies which compartments trigger the tracing:

```{julia}
sirq = OutbreakModel(
    [:S, :I, :R, :Q],
    [false, true, false, false],
    [OutbreakTransition(:S, :I, 1/6, :infection; via = [:I]),
     OutbreakTransition(:I, :R, 0.25, :spontaneous),
     OutbreakTransition(:S, :Q, 0.1, :contact_trace; via = [:I])];
    name = :SIRQ)
```

The `:contact_trace` transition `S → Q` fires at rate 0.1 per
(S-node, I-neighbour) pair — the same edge-counting mechanism as
`:infection`, but moving the susceptible to quarantine instead of
infecting them. This models imperfect tracing: only a fraction of
contacts are traced per unit time.

## Baseline SIR (no tracing)

```{julia}
N = 1000
g = erdos_renyi(N, 5 / (N - 1); rng = StableRNG(42))

sir = OutbreakModel([:S, :I, :R], [false, true, false],
    [OutbreakTransition(:S, :I, 1/6, :infection; via = [:I]),
     OutbreakTransition(:I, :R, 0.25, :spontaneous)]; name = :SIR)

spec_sir = OutbreakSpec(model = sir, network = g,
    initial = SeedFraction(:I => 0.01), tspan = (0.0, 40.0))
ens_sir = simulate_ensemble(spec_sir; nsims = 200, seed = 1, parallel = false)
t_sir, μ_sir = mean_curve(ens_sir, :I)
```

## With contact tracing

```{julia}
spec_ct = OutbreakSpec(model = sirq, network = g,
    initial = SeedFraction(:I => 0.01), tspan = (0.0, 40.0))
ens_ct = simulate_ensemble(spec_ct; nsims = 200, seed = 1, parallel = false)
t_ct, μ_ct = mean_curve(ens_ct, :I)
_, μ_Q = mean_curve(ens_ct, :Q)
```

```{julia}
#| label: fig-contact-tracing
#| fig-cap: "SIR epidemic with and without contact tracing (quarantine rate 0.1 per I-neighbour)."
plot(t_sir, μ_sir ./ N, label = "No tracing", lw = 2, color = :red,
     xlabel = "Time", ylabel = "Fraction",
     title = "Contact tracing: S→Q at rate 0.1 per I-neighbour")
plot!(t_ct, μ_ct ./ N, label = "I (with tracing)", lw = 2, color = :blue)
plot!(t_ct, μ_Q ./ N, label = "Q (quarantined)", lw = 2, ls = :dash, color = :purple)
```

Contact tracing reduces the peak by quarantining susceptible contacts
before they can be infected. The quarantined fraction Q grows as the
epidemic progresses.

## Varying tracing intensity

```{julia}
#| label: fig-tracing-intensity
#| fig-cap: "Higher tracing rates (α) reduce the epidemic more effectively."
p = plot(t_sir, μ_sir ./ N, label = "No tracing", lw = 2, color = :grey,
         xlabel = "Time", ylabel = "Infected fraction",
         title = "Contact tracing intensity")
for (α, col) in [(0.05, :orange), (0.1, :blue), (0.2, :green), (0.5, :purple)]
    m = OutbreakModel([:S, :I, :R, :Q], [false, true, false, false],
        [OutbreakTransition(:S, :I, 1/6, :infection; via = [:I]),
         OutbreakTransition(:I, :R, 0.25, :spontaneous),
         OutbreakTransition(:S, :Q, α, :contact_trace; via = [:I])]; name = :SIRQ)
    spec = OutbreakSpec(model = m, network = g,
        initial = SeedFraction(:I => 0.01), tspan = (0.0, 40.0))
    ens = simulate_ensemble(spec; nsims = 200, seed = 1, parallel = false)
    _, μ = mean_curve(ens, :I)
    plot!(p, t_sir[1:length(μ)], μ ./ N,
          label = "α=$α", lw = 2, color = col)
end
p
```

Higher tracing rates suppress the epidemic more effectively. At α=0.5
(tracing rate 3× the transmission rate), the epidemic is nearly
eliminated.

## Summary

- `:contact_trace` transitions provide rate-based contact tracing
  within the Gillespie SSA framework.
- Each quarantine is an elementary SSA event — no batch state changes.
- The `via` keyword controls which compartments trigger tracing.
- Tracing intensity (rate α) determines effectiveness: α ≈ β
  substantially reduces the epidemic.
